content.full_text:
CITY OF GARDEN GROVE, CALIFORNIA
11391 ACACIA PARKWAY, P.O. BOX 3070, GARDEN GROVE, CALIFORNIA 92642

OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY
(714)741-5367

GARDEN GROVE

March 6, 1990

Joseph Garcia
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street
Sacramento, CA 95874

Subject: Request for Advice Letter Re:
Financial Conflict of Interest
FPPC Reference No. 90-162

Dear Mr. Garcia:

FACTS:

Project No. 735 encompasses Chapman Avenue between Euclid Street
and Ninth Street, a distance of .6 miles, all within the City of
Garden Grove. Chapman Avenue is a main arterial street within the
City of Garden Grove. Chapman Avenue runs east and west and is
being widened from the existing width of 80' to 100' to accommodate more traffic.

The project was approved for funding from the Orange County Transportation Commission in the fiscal year 1988-89. It includes new
paving, street reconstruction, raised medians with irrigation system, street widening, and street lights. I have included a memo
dated February 9, 1990, from Development Services Director, Frank
Schuma, to City Manager, George Tindall, describing the project.

A vote was taken on the awarding of a contract involving Project
No. 735 at the Council meeting of February 20, 1990. I have
enclosed a copy of the Minutes of that Council meeting.

Two Councilmembers, Frank Kessler and J. Tilman Williams,
abstained from voting on the contract award because of the location of their individual residences. I have enclosed two maps
which indicate the residences of the respective Councilmembers.

The project calls for the acquisition of twenty (20) feet of frontage from twelve (12) parcels: 11252, 11272, 11302, 11322, 11352,
11342, 11372, 11382, 11402, 11412, 11432, and 11452 Chapman
Avenue. This will take place by condemnation or acquisition in
the future and will need Council approval.

"PRIDE IN PROGRESS"

Joseph Garcia
March 6, 1990
Page 2

Subject:    Request for Advice Letter Re:
            Financial Conflict of Interest

The Garden Grove City Council consists of four councilmembers and
a mayor, elected at large. Concerning Councilmember, J. Tilman
Williams, who lives at 11241 Chapman Avenue, which is a corner
lot, the property is respectively 80', 290', 665' and 920' from
the properties to be acquired.

Councilmember Frank Kessler lives on a private road at 11352
Bridler Drive. It has no direct access to Chapman Avenue. The
properties fronting Chapman Avenue which will be affected by the
street widening project are 11272 at 470'; 11352 at 370'; 11402 at 425'; and 11432 at 530'.

Councilmember Kessler is 285' from the north curb of Chapman
Avenue at its nearest point. Councilmember Williams' residence is
located on the north side of Chapman Avenue.

ISSUES:

1.  Does the ownership of his residence disqualify Councilmember
    Williams from voting on any or all of the condemnations or
    acquisitions?

2.  Does the ownership of his residence disqualify Councilmember
    Kessler?

Thank you for your assistance in this matter.

Very truly yours,

STUART SCUDDER
City Attorney

By MARILYN WICZYNSKI
   Acting Deputy City Attorney

MW/pw:6255G
Enclosures

AGENDA ITEM No. 6.L.1

City of Garden Grove

INTER- DEPARTMENT MEMORANDUM


To:     George L. Tindall                    From:   Frank A. Schuma

Dept:   City Manager                        Dept:   Development Services

Subject: PUBLIC RIGHT-OF-WAY                Date:   February 9, 1990
         Project No. 735, Chapman Avenue
         Between Euclid Street and Ninth Street
         OCUTT No. GG-89-23
         Recommendation to Award Contract and
         Adopt OCUTT Funding Resolution

BACKGROUND

The Orange County Transportation Commission (OCTC) has approved the City of Garden
Grove's Fiscal Year 88-89 application for Orange County Unified Transportation
Trust (OCUTT) funds for the improvement of Chapman Avenue from Euclid Street to
Ninth Street.

Plans and specifications for the above project were approved by the City Council on
December 18, 1989. The project includes new paving, street reconstruction, raised
landscape medians with irrigation system, street widening and street lights.

The project was authorized in the FY 88-89 budget and is being financed with OCUTT,
Kaiser, and Street Light funds. The funding resolution requests a further
allocation and certifies that OCUTT procedures have been followed.

Nine (9) bids were received and opened in the City Clerk's office at 2 p.m. on
Tuesday, January 30, 1990. A copy of the bid summary is attached.

ANALYSIS

The lowest qualified bidder was Sully-Miller Contracting Company with a total bid
of $598,933.35. This is $150,642.65 or 20.10 percent under the engineer's
estimate. This bid is within the current project budget. The licenses and
references of the contractor have been reviewed and verified by staff, and all
other documentation is in order.

RECOMMENDATION

It is recommended that the City Council award the contract to the Sully-Miller
Contracting Company and authorize the Mayor and City Clerk to execute the agreement
on behalf of the City. It is also recommended that Council adopt the OCUTT funding
resolution for the project.

FRANK A. SCHUMA, Director
Development Services Department

By: Joseph S. Schenk
    Engineering Services Manager

Attachments: Bid Summary
             Resolution

8578R/1837A

RECOMMENDED FOR APPROVAL;

George L. Tindall
City Manager

MINUTES

GARDEN GROVE CITY COUNCIL


A regular meeting of the City Council of the City of Garden Grove

was held in the Council Chamber, 11300 Stanford Avenue, on Tuesday, 10

February 20, 1990, at 7:30 p.m.


ROLL CALL: PRESENT: (5) MAYOR DONOVAN, COUNCILMEMBERS DINEEN,

KESSLER, LITTRELL, WILLIAMS

ABSENT: (0) NONE

ALSO PRESENT: City Manager, George Tindall; Deputy City Manager/

Administrative Services/Community Services Director,

Michael Fenderson; Deputy City Manager, Patrick Importuna;

City Attorney, Stuart Scudder; City Clerk, Carolyn Morris;

Development Services Director, Frank Schuma; Fire Chief,

Lon Cahill; Housing and Neighborhood Development Director,

Gregory Deveraux; Personnel Services Director, Steven Larson;

Police Chief, John Robertson; and Public Services Director,

Richard Conrad.


The invocation was given by Pastor Wynn Nguyen, Vietnamese

It was moved by Councilman Williams, seconded by Councilman
Littrell, and carried by unanimous vote, that a cost recovery
policy for small hydrocarbon materials spills, be and hereby is
approved. Said motion carried by the following vote:

AYES: COUNCILMEMBERS: (5) DINGEN, KESSLER, LITTRELL,
WILLIAMS, DUNOVAN

NOES: COUNCILMEMBERS: (0) NONE

ABSENT: COUNCILMEMBERS: (0) NONE


CLAIMS OF COMPANY X AND FUNDING RESOLUTION FOR PROJECT NO. 289, CHAPMAN
AVENUE IMPROVEMENTS BETWEEN JULY 5TH AND NINTH STREETS (1. 96.PRO1. 735)

The City Attorney stated that Councilmembers Kessler and Williams
cannot vote on this matter inasmuch as they own property in the
immediate vicinity and have a possible conflict of interest.

Councilman Dingen inquired in the event eminent domain proceedings
were needed for the acquisition of the remainder of the properties
necessary for this project, how that would pertain to
Councilmembers Kessler and Williams. The City Attorney explained
the procedure, citing that it was would be drawn and one of the two
Councilmembers would have to vote.

To assist the legal voters presiding in the Orange County News,

January 13-17, 1920, the City Manager advised that name (S) bid

6/20/90

proposals were received and opened January 30, 1930 at 2:00 p.m.,
for the above-titled project.

Alderman Littell moved, seconded by Mayor Donovan, that
City-Miller Contracting Company be and hereby is determined to be
the lowest acceptable bidder in connection with Project No. 735,
Chapman Avenue Improvements between Euclid and Nantih Streets, and
that contract for said project be and hereby is awarded to said
Company in the sum of Five Hundred Ninety-eight Thousand, Nine
Hundred Thirty Three dollars and 50/100 ($598,933.50), in
accordance with the terms of the City's plans and specifications
and the bid submitted by said Company, that all other bids be and
hereby are rejected; and the Mayor is authorized to execute said
contract on behalf of the City of Garden Grove and the City Clerk
is directed to attest to same. Said motion carried by the
following vote:

AYES:      COUNCILMEMBERS: (5) DINSDEN, LITTELL, DONOVAN

NOES:      COUNCILMEMBERS: (0) NONE

ABSTAIN:   COUNCILMEMBERS: (0) NONE

ABSENT:    COUNCILMEMBERS: (2) KLOSKY, WILLIAMS

[illegible] NO. 1946 CC

Councilmen Littrell moved, seconded by Mayor Donovan, that full

reading of "Resolution No. 1207, 50 be waived, and said Resolution

entitled A RESOLUTION OF THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF GARDEN

GROVE REQUESTING THE ORANGE COUNTY TRANSPORTATION COMMISSION TO

ALLOCATE ORANGE COUNTY LIMITED TRANSPORTATION TRUST (OCLT) FUNDS

FOR CHAPMAN AVENUE BETWEEN LOCUST STREET AND NINTH STREET, be and

hereby is adopted. Upon the following vote:

AYES:     COUNCILMEMBERS: (3) DINEEN, LITTRELL, DONOVAN

NOES:     COUNCILMEMBERS: (0) NONE

ABSENT:   COUNCILMEMBERS: (0) NONE

ABSTAIN:  COUNCILMEMBERS: (2) KLESSLER, WILLIAMS


Said Resolution No. 1207-50 was declared adopted.

Re: REQUEST FOR ADVICE LETTER REGARDING FINANCIAL CONFLICT OF
    INTEREST


ISSUE:

Are both councilmembers required to abstain from voting in regards
to a condemnation proceeding for a street widening project near
their individual residences?

FACTS:

Councilman Kessler lives at 11332. He is the furthest from the
condemnation properties that are involved in the street widening.
Please refer to the enclosed map.

INQUIRY:

Under Regulation 18702.3, it would appear that the 2,500 foot
radius which involves an appreciation of $10,000.00 would disqual-
ify him from voting. Is this correct?

FACTS:

Councilman Williams lives at 11241 on the corner and is the clos-
est to the project. Please refer to the enclosed map.

INQUIRY:

Under Regulation 18702.3, it would appear that Mr. Williams' prop-
erty which is less than 300 feet would materially be affected.


"PRIDE IN PROGRESS"

[illegible]

[illegible]

sections.facts:
Project No. 735 encompasses Chapman Avenue between Euclid Street and Ninth Street, a distance of .6 miles, all within the City of Garden Grove. Chapman Avenue is a main arterial street within the City of Garden Grove. Chapman Avenue runs east and west and is being widened from the existing width of 80' to 100' to accommodate more traffic.

The project was approved for funding from the Orange County Transportation Commission in the fiscal year 1988-89. It includes new paving, street reconstruction, raised medians with irrigation system, street widening, and street lights. I have included a memo dated February 9, 1990, from Development Services Director, Frank Schuma, to City Manager, George Tindall, describing the project.

A vote was taken on the awarding of a contract involving Project No. 735 at the Council meeting of February 20, 1990. I have enclosed a copy of the Minutes of that Council meeting.

Two Councilmembers, Frank Kessler and J. Tilman Williams, abstained from voting on the contract award because of the location of their individual residences. I have enclosed two maps which indicate the residences of the respective Councilmembers.

The project calls for the acquisition of twenty (20) feet of frontage from twelve (12) parcels: 11252, 11272, 11302, 11322, 11352, 11342, 11372, 11382, 11402, 11412, 11432, and 11452 Chapman Avenue. This will take place by condemnation or acquisition in the future and will need Council approval.

Subject: Request for Advice Letter Re: Financial Conflict of Interest

The Garden Grove City Council consists of four councilmembers and a mayor, elected at large. Concerning Councilmember, J. Tilman Williams, who lives at 11241 Chapman Avenue, which is a corner lot, the property is respectively 80', 290', 665' and 920' from the properties to be acquired.

Councilmember Frank Kessler lives on a private road at 11352 Bixler Drive. It has no direct access to Chapman Avenue. The properties fronting Chapman Avenue which will be affected by the street widening project are 11252 at 560' feet from Kessler's home 11272 at 470'; 11352 at 370'; 11402 at 425'; and 11432 at 530'.

Councilmember Kessler is 285' from the north curb of Chapman Avenue at its nearest point. Councilmember Williams' residence is located on the north side of Chapman Avenue.

ISSUES:

1. Does the ownership of his residence disqualify Councilmember Williams from voting on any or all of the condemnations or acquisitions?

2. Does the ownership of his residence disqualify Councilmember Kessler?

Thank you for your assistance in this matter.
sections.analysis:
The lowest qualified bidder was Sully-Miller Contracting Company with a total bid of $598,933.35. This is $150,642.65 or 20.10 percent under the engineer's estimate. This bid is within the current project budget. The licenses and references of the contractor have been reviewed and verified by staff, and all other documentation is in order.

RECOMMENDATION

It is recommended that the City Council award the contract to the Sully-Miller Contracting Company and authorize the Mayor and City Clerk to execute the agreement on behalf of the City. It is also recommended that Council adopt the OCUTT funding resolution for the project.

FRANK A. SCHUMA, Director
Development Services Department
By: Joseph S. Schenk
Engineering Services Manager

RECOMMENDED FOR APPROVAL:
George L. Tindall
City Manager

This document is a bid summary sheet for the City of Garden Grove's Development Services Department, specifically for the project "Chapman Avenue - From Euclid Street to Ninth Street." The sheet lists various items of work with their quantities, units, and estimated costs from different contractors: Engineer's Estimate, Fecit Strata Const., Griffith Company, and Damon Const.

Here is a breakdown of the columns:

- Item: Numbered list of work items.
- Description of Work: Brief description of each item.
- Quan.: Quantity of each item.
- Unit: Unit of measurement (e.g., EA., L.S., C.Y., S.Y., L.F., EA.).
- Engineer's Estimate: Estimated cost per unit and total cost.
- Fecit Strata Const.: Estimated cost per unit and total cost.
- Griffith Company: Estimated cost per unit and total cost.
- Damon Const.: Estimated cost per unit and total cost.

The final row shows the total costs for each contractor and the percentage under/over the Engineer's Estimate.

For example:
- Item 20: Adjust Manhole to Grade
  - Quan.: EA. 12
  - Unit: $325.00
  - Engineer's Estimate: $3,900.00
  - Fecit Strata Const.: $2,400.00
  - Griffith Company: $4,200.00
  - Damon Const.: $4,560.00

The total costs for all items are as follows:
- Engineer's Estimate: $749,576.00
- Fecit Strata Const.: $651,841.90
- Griffith Company: $662,115.25
- Damon Const.: $755,251.50

The percentages under/over the Engineer's Estimate are:
- Engineer's Estimate: 13.04% Under
- Fecit Strata Const.: 11.67% Under
- Griffith Company: 1.16% Over

MINUTES
GARDEN GROVE CITY COUNCIL

A regular meeting of the City Council of the City of Garden Grove was held in the Council Chamber, 11300 Stanford Avenue, on Tuesday, February 20, 1990, at 7:30 p.m.

ROLL CALL: PRESENT: (5) MAYOR DONOVAN, COUNCILMEMBERS DINSEN, KESSLER, LITTRELL, WILLIAMS

ABSENT: (0) NONE

ALSO PRESENT: City Manager, George Tindall; Deputy City Manager/Administrative Services/Community Services Director, Michael Fenderson; Deputy City Manager, Patrick Importuna; City Attorney, Stuart Scudder; City Clerk, Carolyn Morris; Development Services Director, Frank Schuma; Fire Chief, Lon Cahill; Housing and Neighborhood Development Director, Gregory Devereaux; Personnel Services Director, Steven Larson; Police Chief, John Robertson; and Public Services Director, Richard Conrad.

The Invocation was given by Pastor Wynn Nguyen, Vietnamese

It was moved by Councilman Williams, seconded by Councilman Littrell, and carried by unanimous vote, that a cost recovery policy for small hydrocarbon materials spills, be and hereby is approved. Said motion carried by the following vote:

AYES: COUNCILMEMBERS: (5) DINSEN, KESSLER, LITTRELL, WILLIAMS, DONOVAN

NOES: COUNCILMEMBERS: (0) NONE

ABSENT: COUNCILMEMBERS: (0) NONE

AWARD OF CONTRACT X AND FUNDING RESOLUTION FOR PROJECT NO. 735, CHAPMAN AVENUE IMPROVEMENTS BETWEEN EUCALYPTUS AND NINTH STREETS (V: 96.Proj. 735)

The City Attorney stated that Councilmembers Kessler and Williams cannot vote on this matter inasmuch as they own property in the immediate vicinity and have a possible conflict of interest.

Councilman Dinsen inquired in the event eminent domain proceedings were needed for the acquisition of the remainder of the properties necessary for this project, how that would pertain to Councilmembers Kessler and Williams. The City Attorney explained the procedure, noting that lots would be drawn and one of the two Councilmembers would have to vote.

Pursuant to Legal Notice published in the Orange County News,

On January 12-17, 1990, the City Manager advised that nine (9) bid

Proposals were received and opened January 30, 1990 at 2:00 p.m., for the above-titled project.

Councilman Littrell moved, seconded by Mayor Donovan, that Dolly-Miller Contracting Company be and hereby is determined to be the lowest acceptable bidder in connection with Project No. 735, Chapman Avenue Improvements between Euclid and Ninth Streets, and that contract for said project be and hereby is awarded to said company in the sum of Five Hundred Ninety-eight Thousand, Nine Hundred Thirty-three dollars and 35/100 ($598,933.35), in accordance with the terms of the City's plans and specifications and the bid submitted by said company; that all other bids be and hereby are rejected; and the Mayor is authorized to execute said contract on behalf of the City of Garden Grove and the City Clerk is directed to attest to same. Said motion carried by the following vote:

AYES: COUNCILMEMBERS: (3) DINGEN, LITTRELL, DONOVAN
NOES: COUNCILMEMBERS: (0) NONE
ABSENT: COUNCILMEMBERS: (0) NONE
ABSTAIN: COUNCILMEMBERS: (2) KLOSSLER, WILLIAMS

Councilman Littrell moved, seconded by Mayor Donovan, that full reading of Resolution No. 7207-90 be waived, and said Resolution entitled A RESOLUTION OF THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF GARDEN GROVE REQUESTING THE ORANGE COUNTY TRANSPORTATION COMMISSION TO ALLOCATE ORANGE COUNTY UNIFIED TRANSPORTATION TRUST (OCUtt) FUNDS FOR CHAPMAN AVENUE BETWEEN EUCALYPT STREET AND NINTH STREET, be and hereby is adopted. Upon the following vote.

AYES: COUNCILMEMBERS: (3) DINSEN, LITTRELL, DONOVAN
NOES: COUNCILMEMBERS: (0) NONE
ABSENT: COUNCILMEMBERS: (0) NONE
ABSTAIN: COUNCILMEMBERS: (2) KESSLER, WILLIAMS

said Resolution No. 7207-90 was declared adopted.

APPLICATION FOR FUNDING FROM THE CALIFORNIA HOUSING REHABILITATION PROGRAM FOR OWNER OCCUPIED HOUSING - RESOLUTION NO. 7208-90 (F: H.117.4)

Staff report dated February 8, 1990 was introduced.

RESOLUTION NO. 7208-90

Councilman Williams moved, seconded by Councilman Kessler, that full reading of Resolution No. 7208-90 be waived, and said Resolution entitled A RESOLUTION OF THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY

February 22, 1990

Legal Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission


Re: REQUEST FOR ADVICE LETTER REGARDING FINANCIAL CONFLICT OF INTEREST

ISSUE:
Are both councilmembers required to abstain from voting in regards to a condemnation proceeding for a street widening project near their individual residences?

FACTS:
Councilman Kessler lives at 11332. He is the furthest from the condemnation properties that are involved in the street widening. Please refer to the enclosed map.

INQUIRY:
Under Regulation 18702.3, it would appear that the 2,500 foot radius which involves an appreciation of $10,000.00 would disqualify him from voting. Is this correct?

FACTS:
Councilman Williams lives at 11241 on the corner and is the closest to the project. Please refer to the enclosed map.

INQUIRY:
Under Regulation 18702.3, it would appear that Mr. Williams' property which is less than 300 feet would materially be affected.

The image appears to be extremely overexposed, making it difficult to read or interpret any specific content. There is very little visible detail due to the high brightness and lack of contrast. If you have another scan or a higher quality image, please provide it for better results.

embedding.qa_text: March 6, 1990 Joseph Garcia Fair Political Practices Commission 428 "J" Street Sacramento, CA 95874 Subject: Request for Advice Letter Re: Financial Conflict of Interest FPPC Reference No. 90-162 Dear Mr. Garcia: FACTS: Project No. 735 encompasses Chapman Avenue between Euclid Street and Ninth Street, a distance of .6 miles, all within the City of Garden Grove. Chapman Avenue is a main arterial street within the City of Garden Grove. Chapman Avenue runs east and west and is being widened from the existing width of 80' to 100' to accommodate more traffic. The project was approved for funding from the Orange County Transportation Commission in the fiscal year 1988-89. It includes new paving, street reconstruction, raised medians with irrigation system, street widening, and street lights. I have included a memo dated February 9, 1990, from Development Services Director, Frank Schuma, to City Manager, George Tindall, describing the project. A vote was taken on the awarding of a contract involving Project No. 735 at the Council meeting of February 20, 1990. I have enclosed a copy of the Minutes of that Council meeting. Two Councilmembers, Frank Kessler and J. Tilman Williams, abstained from voting on the contract award because of the location of their individual residences. I have enclosed two maps which indicate the residences of the respective Councilmembers. The project calls for the acquisition of twenty (20) feet of frontage from twelve (12) parcels: 11252, 11272, 11302, 11322, 11352, 11342, 11372, 11382, 11402, 11412, 11432, and 11452 Chapman Avenue. This will take place by condemnation or acquisition in the future and will need Council approval. Subject: Request for Advice Letter Re: Financial Conflict of Interest The Garden Grove City Council consists of four councilmembers and a mayor, elected at large. Concerning Councilmember, J. Tilman Williams, who lives at 11241 Chapman Avenue, which is a corner lot, the property is respectively 80', 290', 665' and 920' from the properties to be acquired. Councilmember Frank Kessler lives on a private road at 11352 Bixler Drive. It has no direct access to Chapman Avenue. The properties fronting Chapman Avenue which will be affected by the street widening project are 11252 at 560' feet from Kessler's home 11272 at 470'; 11352 at 370'; 11402 at 425'; and 11432 at 530'. Councilmember Kessler is 285' from the north curb of Chapman Avenue at its nearest point. Councilmember Williams' residence is located on the north side of Chapman Avenue. ISSUES: 1. Does the ownership of his residence disqualify Councilmember Williams from voting on any or all of the condemnations or acquisitions? 2. Does the ownership of his residence disqualify Councilmember Kessler? Thank you for your assistance in this matter. Very truly yours, STUART SCUDDER City Attorney By MARILYN WICZYNSKI Acting Deputy City Attorney MW/pw:6255G Enclosures **AGENDA ITEM No. 6.b.1** City of Garden Grove INTER-DEPARTMENT MEMORANDUM To: George L. Tindall Dept: City Manager Subject: PUBLIC RIGHT-OF-WAY Project No. 735, Chapman Avenue Between Euclid Street and Ninth Street OCUTT No. GG-89-23 Recommendation to Award Contract and Adopt OCUTT
